revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Chiba (Klan)]][[en:Chiba clan]][[fa:خاندان چیبا]][[fr:Clan Chiba]][[it:Clan Chiba]][[ja:千葉氏]][[pt:Clã Chiba]][[zh:千叶氏]]
{{Short description|Japanese clan|qq=}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname = Chiba |surname nihongo = 千葉 |image = image:Japanese crest Tsuki ni Hoshi.svg |image size = 140px |image caption = ''Gessei'', the [[Mon (emblem)|mon]] of the Chiba clan |home province = {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|qid=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa|1=Shimōsa Province}} |parent house = [[Taira clan]] |titles = {{ill|Shugo|en|Shugo|qid=Q1141023|lt=Shugo|1=Shugo}} |founder = [[Chiba Tsunetane]] |founding year = 12th century |ruled until = 16th century |cadet branches = [[Sōma clan]], [[Tō clan]] }} The '''Chiba clan''' (千葉氏 ''Chiba-shi'') was a Japanese ''[[gōzoku]]'' and {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|qid=Q38142|lt=Samurai|1=Samurai}} family descending from the [[Taira clan]]. The clan was founded by [[Chiba Tsunetane]], the son of {{ill|Taira no Tadatsune|en|Taira no Tadatsune|qid=Q1105844|lt=Taira no Tadatsune|1=Taira no Tadatsune}}. The Chiba governed in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|qid=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}}, and the clan was based in present-day [[:en:Chiba, Chiba|Chiba City]]. Additionally, for a period, the clan controlled the [[Sōma clan|Sōma Manor]] that extended into present-day {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. After the establishment of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|qid=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}, the head of the Chiba clan became the hereditary {{ill|Shugo|en|Shugo|qid=Q1141023|lt=Shugo|1=Shugo}} governor of Shimōsa Province.<ref>{{cite book | last = Sansom | first = George | title = A history of Japan to 1334 | url = https://archive.org/details/historyofjapanto00sans | url-access = registration | publisher = Stanford University Press | location = Stanford, Calif | year = 1958 | isbn = 978-0-8047-0523-3 |qq=}}</ref> == Origin == The Chiba clan descends from the 8th century [[Emperor Kanmu]] through the sequence of [[Imperial Prince Kazurahara]] (786-853) — [[Prince Takami]] — {{ill|Taira no Takamochi|en|Taira no Takamochi|qid=Q11483791|lt=Taira no Takamochi|1=Taira no Takamochi}} — [[Muraoka Yoshifumi]] — [[Muraoka Tadayori]] — [[Chiba Tadatsune]] — [[Chiba Tsunemasa]] — [[Chiba Tsunenaga]] — [[Chiba Tsunekane]] — {{ill|Chiba Tsuneshige|en|Chiba Tsuneshige|qid=Q7676359|lt=Chiba Tsuneshige|1=Chiba Tsuneshige}} — [[Chiba Tsunetane]] — [[Azuma Taneyori]] . The {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|qid=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}} authorized the head of Chiba family, [[Chiba Sadatane]], as chief ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' and {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|qid=Q38142|lt=Samurai|1=Samurai}} of the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}}. The clan settled in the Shimōsa area in the early 12th century. The Chiba came into conflict with {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitomo|en|Minamoto no Yoshitomo|qid=Q532301|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitomo|1=Minamoto no Yoshitomo}} during the 1140s over estates in present-day {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Chiba_clan |title=Chiba clan - SamuraiWiki |website=wiki.samurai-archives.com |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130609030437/http://wiki.samurai-archives.com/index.php?title=Chiba_clan |archive-date=2013-06-09|qq=}} </ref> The Chiba, however, came to support Yoshitomo in the [[:en:Hōgen Rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]] (1156). == Genpei War == During the {{ill|Genpei War|en|Genpei War|qid=Q737542|lt=Genpei War|1=Genpei War}} (1180–1185) the Chiba clan, as well as the {{ill|Hōjō clan|en|Hōjō clan|qid=Q834364|lt=Hōjō|1=Hōjō clan}}, {{ill|Miura clan|en|Miura clan|qid=Q2053762|lt=Miura|1=Miura clan}}, and [[Doi clan|Doi]] clans, opposed the greater 'core' Taira clan and supported [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]]. Chiba Tsunetane, clan chief of the period, won Yoritomo’s trust and helped establish the Kamakura shogunate. The power of the clan increased in this period, but ultimately declined during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}} (1336–1573). == Later history == The Chiba clan was completely conquered by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} before the establishment of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. Many descendants of the Chiba clan live in Chiba Prefecture today. Chiba Castle, reconstructed in 1967, is built on the site of {{ill|Inohana Castle|en|Inohana Castle|qid=Q28692626|lt=Inohana Castle|1=Inohana Castle}}, an area previously controlled by the clan.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.ccb.or.jp/e/_sightseeing/2262|title=Chiba Convention Bureauand International Center (CCB-IC)｜Chiba Meetings Industry Information Website|qq=}}</ref> == References == {{Reflist|qq=}}{{Myōken Faith|qq=}}
[[en:Chiba clan]]
